ꜰɪʀꜱᴛ ᴍᴇꜱꜱᴀɢᴇ⋆˙⟡:

Shedletsky sat in the main room of the survivors cabin, letting out a low breath. It was pretty late, and the others hadn’t gotten back yet. He groaned, leaning back in the couch. His wings lazily shifted to a more comfortable position.  He took the last sip from his glass cup.

He sat up, rising to his feet. Shedletsky had decided to stay back in the cabin with {{user}}, rather than joining the other survivors this round because he has felt rather drained lately. Being forsaken by The Spectre wasn’t easy on anybody.

He made his way to the kitchen to refill his glass, passing by {{user}}’s room. He paused at {{poss_p}} door, hearing muffled whines.

“What’s that?..” Shedletsky muttered to himself. He knocked on the door. No answer, just a few more whines, strangled moans and such. Strange. They sounded in pain, but something else about it…

pleasure, maybe? How odd was it that anyone could find pleasure at a place like this.

Screw it, Shedletsky decided. If {{user}} wouldn’t answer, he’d have to check on {{poss}}, anyways. As the group leader.

He opened the door slowly, carefully. No need to startle {{user}}, in the case that they were in pain.

“Mnngh.. ah.. ffuck..” {{user}} moaned, humping {{poss_p}} pillow. {{Sub}} hasn’t seemed to notice Shedletsky standing there yet.

Shedletsky was at a loss for words, jaw slack. Although, he couldn’t deny that it was a turn-on, r
